title: KVRouteValue
description: The value stored at KV key `route:<host>` and read by the serving Worker. This is the single cross-language data contract between the Go API (the only writer) and the Cloudflare Worker (read-only). Postgres is authoritative; this projection is fully rebuildable.

org_id.description: Owning org (identity.organization.id / app.org_meta.id). Used to build the per-org R2 blob prefix.

version_id.description: The live, immutable version to serve (app.site_versions.id). Pointer flips on publish/rollback.

access_mode.description: How the Worker gates this host. Phase 1 implements `public` only; the rest are Phase 2.

schema_version.description: Version of THIS contract shape. Lets the Worker reject/upgrade projections written by an older/newer Go API. Bump on any breaking change to this schema. v2 (Phase 2) added the optional expires_at field; v3 added the optional plan_tier field; v4 added the optional chat_id field. The parser accepts v1 through v4.

expires_at.description: OPTIONAL (v2+). RFC3339 timestamp after which the Worker refuses to serve this host — public/unlisted link expiry enforced at the edge. Absent → no edge expiry. Identity-gated (allowlist/org_only) expiry is refused at mint time in the Go API instead.

plan_tier.description: OPTIONAL (v3+). The owning org's plan tier (app.org_meta.plan_tier, e.g. "free"/"pro"). The Worker uses it to decide whether to inject the free-tier "Deployed with Dropway" attribution banner. Absent → tier unknown → no banner.

chat_id.description: OPTIONAL (v4+). The id of the site's attached, panel-enabled chat log (Share This Session). When present, the Worker injects the "How this was made" pill into served HTML and serves the compiled transcript at the reserved /__dropway/chat path (chat-transcripts/<org_id>/<chat_id>.json in the content bucket). Absent → no chat surface.
